content.text:
All three files have been created successfully. Here is a summary of what was written:

---

### File 1: `C:\Users\User\nexus-ai\supabase\migrations\001_initial_schema.sql`

Complete Supabase database migration with:

**Tables (9 total):**
- `profiles` -- extends `auth.users` with org membership and role
- `organizations` -- team workspaces with Stripe billing and plan quotas
- `documents` -- uploaded files with processing status tracking
- `extraction_templates` -- field definitions for document extraction (invoice, contract, receipt, custom)
- `surveys` -- survey definitions with JSONB questions and settings
- `survey_responses` -- respondent answers with optional email and metadata
- `analyses` -- cached AI analysis results (themes, sentiment, summary, full)
- `api_keys` -- API key management with hashed keys and expiry
- `activity_log` -- audit trail for document, survey, and template actions

**Key features:**
- UUID primary keys using `gen_random_uuid()`
- Foreign keys with `ON DELETE CASCADE` / `ON DELETE SET NULL` as appropriate
- CHECK constraints on all status/enum fields (`role`, `plan`, `status`, `category`, `analysis_type`, `resource_type`)
- 11 indexes covering org lookups, status filtering, and key hash searches
- Helper function `is_org_member(org_id)` for org-based RLS isolation
- Trigger `on_auth_user_created` to auto-create a profile row when a user signs up
- Trigger `on_survey_response_created` to increment `response_count` on the parent survey
- RLS enabled on all 9 tables with 20 policies covering org isolation, owner/admin permissions, and public survey response submission
- Storage buckets (`documents`, `exports`) with RLS policies keyed on org ID in the folder path

### File 2: `C:\Users\User\nexus-ai\middleware.ts`

Next.js edge middleware that delegates to `@/lib/supabase/middleware` for Supabase auth session management. The matcher excludes static assets, images, and favicon from middleware processing.

### File 3: `C:\Users\User\nexus-ai\src\app\layout.tsx`

Root layout updated from the default Next.js scaffold to include:
- `Inter` font from Google Fonts with CSS variable `--font-sans`
- `ThemeProvider` from `next-themes` with system theme detection and class-based dark mode
- `Toaster` component from `@/components/ui/sonner` for toast notifications
- Rich SEO metadata with title template, description, and keywords
- `suppressHydrationWarning` on `<html>` for theme provider compatibility
